title: Dendrocousinsia howardiana
taxonomy: Dendrocousinsia howardiana is classified within the plant family Dipterocarpaceae. It is a species of tree native to Asia and belongs to the genus Dendrocousinsia. The classification of Dendrocousinsia howardiana is further divided into the kingdom Plantae, followed by the division Magnoliophyta and class Magnoliopsida. The order Malvales, family Dipterocarpaceae, and genus Dendrocousinsia provide a more detailed classification of this species. This classification is based on the plant's physical characteristics and evolutionary history.
uses: Dendrocousinsia howardiana, also known as the Howard's Dendrocousinsia, is a plant species with potential medicinal properties. The plant's leaves and bark are traditionally used in various herbal remedies. However, further research is needed to confirm the efficacy and safety of using Dendrocousinsia howardiana for medicinal purposes. Preliminary studies suggest that the plant may have anti-inflammatory and antioxidant effects. More studies are necessary to fully understand the plant's medicinal uses and potential benefits.
properties: Dendrocousinsia howardiana, a plant species, is known to have various therapeutic properties, although limited research has been conducted on its specific uses.
